item.title: What Donald Trump Really Loves
item.description: Flattery paves the road to the president’s heart.

item.title: Parents Can’t Give Their Kids Everything. Siblings Can Help.
item.description: There are virtues in growing up in a pack.

item.title: The Trump Vibe Shift Was Just a Mirage
item.description: The country does not love its president.

item.title: Fine, Don’t Prosecute the President. But Release Jack Smith’s Report.
item.description: Its suppression is highly irregular and disturbing.

item.title: A.I. Is Strangling Our Economy
item.description: So much money is being poured into A.I. that the rest of the economy is starting to suffer.

item.title: We Crunched the Data: There’s a Grocery Price Emergency in America
item.description: We dug into the data, toured the country and ran surveys to find the biggest financial pressures people feel.

item.title: We’re in a Golden Age of Queer Literature
item.description: After decades in which Queer literature was the underdog, it is now publishing’s golden goose.

item.title: Trump’s Science Cuts Betray America’s Past Since Lewis and Clark
item.description: America has funded scientific research since 1804 — and remembers that first project as a national glory. Cutting similar funding today is a betrayal.

item.title: Trump Is Turning Journalists Into Criminals
item.description: The product of journalism enjoys substantial protection under the First Amendment. But what about the process?

item.title: Three Opinion Writers on Trump’s Latest Face-Plant
item.description: Trump planned a big party for America. It’s not going well.

item.title: Addressing a Corrupt Pardon System
item.description: Readers respond to “There’s Actually Something We Can Do About Trump’s Pardons,” an Opinion guest essay by Tim Wu. Also: A loophole that harms our oceans.

item.title: Beware the Purging of General Donahue
item.description: The dangers of Pete Hegseth are mounting.

item.title: If These Celebrities Are Democracy’s Last Hope, We’re in Trouble
item.description: When speaking up actually means risking something, for once, it is more necessary than ever.

item.title: The Hamptons’ Essential Luxury Good: Human Labor
item.description: The people who want to visit can’t function without the people who have to live there.

item.title: The Real A.I. Race Isn’t America vs. China
item.description: Washington is starting to follow a similar playbook to Beijing when it comes to artificial intelligence policy.

item.title: It’s Time to Put This Catholic Teaching to Rest
item.description: Pope Leo’s first encyclical takes on a major flaw of Catholic just war theory: its use as a fig leaf for attacking an enemy.

item.title: The Founder We Need Is the One We Don’t Remember
item.description: From 1776 to Jan. 6, one question remains. Which mob should rule?

item.title: We’re Getting America’s Children Hooked on Gambling
item.description: In part because gambling ads are in everything, everywhere, all at once.

item.title: See the Music, Hear the Sights
item.description: I thought it was cheesy to project images during a concert, but it can amplify the experience.

item.title: How the Internet Polarizes Men and Women
item.description: Is the internet polarizing the sexes? On “Interesting Times,” the author Louise Perry argues that our modern connectivity is exposing the previously private worlds of vulgar locker room talk and unkind gossip — and is driving young men and women apart.

item.title: Why Was Keir Starmer So Disastrously Ineffective?
item.description: His great achievement was to restore the Labour Party to government. His lack of conviction made him ineffective once he got there.

item.title: Has the MAGA ‘Sugar Rush’ Finally Run Out?
item.description: Three Opinion writers on Trump’s latest face-plant.

item.title: It’s Ugly. It Costs $640,000. Everyone Is Mad About It but Me.
item.description: Ferrari’s new E.V. is being panned by critics, but it ought to be celebrated.

item.title: Why Old People Cry
item.description: These days, I have so much past behind and within me, it’s as if it bubbles over.

item.title: In Cuba, Where So Many Have Left, These Artists Have Stayed
item.description: Four young Cubans grapple with what it means to stay on an island facing unprecedented migration.

item.title: Anger Over Supreme Court Rulings on Immigration
item.description: Readers are dismayed by the justices’ decisions. Also: The Huntington’s dilemma; a woman’s age; students, cheating and A.I.

item.title: A Bold Idea to Open Up Elite Colleges
item.description: Wanting to make top schools look less like country clubs and more like the country shouldn’t be a partisan issue.

item.title: ICE Is a Job Killer Everywhere It Goes
item.description: ICE surges are costing Americans jobs.

item.title: Why Is the Supreme Court Unshackling the Presidency?
item.description: Where is Chief Justice John Roberts? And why isn’t he putting limits on this administration?

item.title: La Retaguardia (The Ones Who Stay)
item.description: Four young Cubans grapple with what it means to stay on an island facing unprecedented migration.

item.title: Supporting Iran in the World Cup? It’s Complicated.
item.description: Is the team against the regime or an embodiment of it?

item.title: Kevin Warsh Is Missing Alan Greenspan’s Point
item.description: The Fed chairman, Kevin Warsh, shouldn’t point to Alan Greenspan’s experience to bolster his argument that A.I. will allow the central bank to keep interest rates low.

item.title: There’s One Clear Reason Americans Are Gloomy About A.I.
item.description: The key is understanding how our labor market and our social net differ from the rest of the world’s.

item.title: The Supreme Court’s TPS Decision Is a Slap in the Face to Lawful Immigrants
item.description: The Supreme Court just gave the Trump administration free rein to end Temporary Protected Status.

item.title: One-Party Rule Is Hiding in Plain Sight
item.description: Why does our seemingly competitive political system produce so much stagnation and corruption?

item.title: Democrats Are Done With Caution
item.description: Tuesday’s primary demonstrated the astonishing political power of Mamdani and of the Democratic Socialists of America.

item.title: The ‘Rising Tide’ of America’s Middle Class
item.description: Readers respond to an Opinion guest essay, “What Liberals Get Wrong About the Middle Class.” Also: Israel’s isolated artists; the true America.

item.title: Triumph of the Tasteless
item.description: This is an era of illusion, and Musk and Trump are the self-adoring, self-promoting emblems and emperors of it.

item.title: Is It Time for a New Sexual Revolution?
item.description: One that actually brings men and women together.

item.title: Is it Time for a New Sexual Revolution?
item.description: One that actually brings men and women together.

item.title: The Debate Over Remote Work Isn’t Over
item.description: Readers respond to an Opinion guest essay, “We Liked Remote Work Until We Looked at the Data.” Also: The blame game in America today.

item.title: 7 Lessons and Consequences of the Iran War
item.description: Green energy, modern war and American hegemony

item.title: President Narcissus and the Fetid Reflecting Pool
item.description: Why the president’s latest renovation project is so irresistible and resonant.

item.title: Thomas L. Friedman on the Clash at the Core of the Iran Deal
item.description: How the world views of Jared Kushner and the founder of the Islamic Republic of Iran can help explain the issue at the heart of the negotiations.

item.title: ‘I Love Boosters’ Is the Perfect Film for Our Savage Era
item.description: “I Love Boosters” is a brilliant sendup of the absurd contradictions of our age.

item.title: U.S. Support Is Israel’s True Weakness
item.description: Israel’s dependence on the United States has allowed it to ignore the need for diplomacy.

item.title: If You Love America, Cringe for It
item.description: Let’s remember who we once were, because it’s what we may yet be again.

item.title: What’s the Matter With Congress?
item.description: A debate between old friends about whether it is fear or polarization that is making Congress weak.

item.title: The Pool That Reflects Trump’s Presidency
item.description: Readers respond to articles about the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. Also: The politics of fine dining; learning something new.

item.title: How Tehran Won the World
item.description: Iran’s defiance in the face of Western aggression has become a rallying cry.

item.title: Elon Musk Is Too Rich. We’ve Known That Since Plato.
item.description: The philosopher made a radical proposal about wealth.

item.title: Creature From the Green Lagoon
item.description: Trump’s slimy, stinky swamp within the swamp.

item.title: My Father’s Death Was the Start of My Life
item.description: It was the worst thing that ever happened to me. It also taught me everything I needed to live.

item.title: Trump Was Right. The War Ended With a Surrender.
item.description: Each generation seems destined to learn anew the lesson that when hawks declare that a war will be painless, beware.

item.title: We Need to Keep Funding Weird Science
item.description: Science turned a serendipitous finding about lizard venom into one of the most important drugs of the century, but that type of research is getting harder to do.

item.title: In Britain, the Far Right Is Moving to the Mainstream
item.description: Its opponents urgently need to demonstrate that they can do more than throw up their hands in horror.

item.title: The Verdict on Biden Is In
item.description: Preliminary judgments on Joe Biden’s one-term presidency, including Jill Biden’s, are all the rage.

item.title: We Ran the Numbers. Remote Work Is Bad for Us.
item.description: Despite its advantages, work-from-home arrangements have significantly deepened Americans’ isolation and distress.

item.title: Why the Knicks Really Won
item.description: Teamwork isn’t just useful, the Knicks showed. It’s a higher calling.

item.title: JD Vance on the Morality of the Trump Administration
item.description: I asked the vice president what is Christian about this White House.

item.title: The Real Reason Trump Never Stops Talking About Voter Fraud
item.description: “Voter fraud” is not about fraud. It is about who votes and how.

item.title: Dear A.I. Companies, the Doom Trolling Needs to Stop
item.description: The major A.I. companies keep telling us how dangerous their new models can be, yet they must keep on building them.

item.title: Bob Dylan and Liza Minnelli Already Turned 80. They Have Thoughts for Trump.
item.description: “I feel the same as I did 50 years ago,” President Trump said. “It’s crazy.”

item.title: Trump Lost The War He Started In Iran
item.description: The United States is emerging weaker — militarily, diplomatically and economically — than at the start of the war.

item.title: The U.S. Is the Future of Soccer, for Better or Worse
item.description: Thanks to immigrants and the internet, Americans gained entry to a universe beyond our borders.

item.title: One City Might Have Just Cracked the Housing Crisis
item.description: A large real estate development in the middle of Vancouver, British Columbia, shows how cities can build more housing.

item.title: Detroit Is the Last City That Knows How to Dance
item.description: Dance floors are flooded with selfies and poseurs. But in one city, it’s still a place to feel free.

item.title: Does U.F.O. Disclosure Threaten Faith?
item.description: Steven Spielberg’s ‘Disclosure Day’ and the God-and-aliens debate.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein Haunts the White House
item.description: A sleazy situation that Trump can’t wish away.
